data.title: Yoon Suk Yeol Faces Five-Year Sentence Request in Escape-Aid Trial
data.description:
According to Lianhe Zaobao citing Yonhap, a Seoul Central District Court panel held the closing hearing on Friday, July 24, in the first trial of former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol, who is accused of helping former Defense Minister Lee Jong-sup flee overseas. Prosecutors asked the court to sentence Yoon to five years in prison. They also sought three-year prison terms each for former National Security Office chief Cho Tae-yong and former Justice Minister Park Sung-jae, who were indicted alongside Yoon.

The special counsel said Yoon used state power for private and political ends, obstructed criminal justice procedures, rendered justice ineffective and infringed on the state’s investigative authority. The case stems from the July 2023 death of Marine Corps Lance Corporal Cha, who died while taking part in flood relief work in North Gyeongsang Province. The special counsel said Yoon became angry on July 31 after receiving an initial finding from the Marine Corps investigation team that upper-level commanders should be held responsible. It said Lee and other Defense Ministry and presidential office officials abused their power to pressure investigators, stop them from transferring the case to police and order changes to related records. On November 19, 2023, Yoon nominated Lee as ambassador to Australia, which the special counsel said was intended to help him flee overseas.

data.title: STOCKS | Novo Nordisk Seeks Preliminary Injunction Against Eli Lilly GLP-1 Ads
data.description: According to Jin10, Novo Nordisk (NVO.N) said it filed a motion for a preliminary injunction in the U.S. District Court in New Jersey, seeking an immediate halt to Eli Lilly's (LLY.N) misleading GLP-1 advertising campaign while its lawsuit continues.

data.title: STOCKS | Ortega Buys Paris Office Complex for About €800 Million
data.description: Amancio Ortega bought a business center in Paris from Invesco Ltd. for about €800 million, his biggest transaction in Europe, according to Bloomberg. The buyer is the Zara billionaire, and the deal adds another large property asset to his portfolio.

data.title: STOCKS | Hong Kong First-Quarter Life Insurance New Policy Premiums Rise 51.1%
data.description:
The Insurance Authority said first-quarter provisional statistics showed life insurance new policy premiums rose 51.1% year on year to HK$141.1 billion, according to Ming Pao.

New policy premiums from non-linked individual life insurance business increased 50.2% to HK$135.3 billion. Within that, participating business rose 53.7% to HK$125.7 billion, while other business climbed to HK$9.7 billion. New policy premiums from investment-linked individual business rose 77.2% to HK$5.7 billion. Insurers issued about 28,000 qualified deferred annuity policies in the quarter, with premiums of HK$1.8 billion, accounting for 1.3% of total individual business.

data.title: China Growth ETF Falls as Alphabet Lifts 2026 Capex Guidance
data.description:
China’s ChiNext Index fell 2.65% as of the close on July 24, 2026, while Huaxia ChiNext ETF (159957) lost 2.46% with turnover of 10.91% and trading volume of 286 million yuan. The ChiNext Momentum Growth Index slipped 2.18%, and Huaxia ChiNext Growth ETF (159967) fell 2.09% with turnover of 18.08% and trading volume of 1.078 billion yuan, according to Jiemian News.

Jiemian News reported that Alphabet said second-quarter 2026 capital expenditure reached $44.9 billion, double a year earlier, and raised full-year guidance to $195 billion to $205 billion, mainly for AI infrastructure expansion. Guangda Securities said Google Cloud revenue rose 82% year on year and backlog reached $514 billion, underscoring strong demand for computing power. It added that servers, AI chips, optical modules, power supplies and liquid cooling are likely to benefit first from earlier equipment delivery.

Separately, Shenwan Hongyuan Research said China’s “fixed income plus” fund size approached 3 trillion yuan in the second quarter of 2026, led by hybrid bond funds, which added more than 300 billion yuan in the quarter. It said technology accounted for 45% of top holdings in such funds, with electronics the main area of accumulation, while cyclical sectors were cut by 9% to 14%. New fixed income plus fund launches in June reached 33.2 billion yuan, the highest in nearly two years.

data.title: South Korean Court Upholds FIU Ban on Unlicensed Stablecoin Exchange Services
data.description: A South Korean court ruled that the FIU lawfully barred unlicensed exchange services for stablecoins and other virtual assets for foreign customers. According to NS3.AI, the Seoul Administrative Court dismissed Darwin KS's lawsuit on July 24. The court said coin exchange services must comply with the virtual asset service provider license requirement.

data.title: Iran's Revolutionary Guards Release Footage of Advanced Heavy Attack Drones
data.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards released footage of advanced heavy attack drones launched in the 27th wave of the "Nasr 2" operation targeting U.S. ammunition depots and personnel hangars in Kuwait.

data.title: Iran's Judiciary Chief Ejei: U.S. Must Drop Excessive Demands and Bullying
data.description: According to Jin10, Iran's Judiciary Chief Ejei said the only way out for the United States and its president is to abandon excessive demands and bullying, and never imagine that the Iranian people will submit.

data.title: Mitsubishi UFJ: Rising Energy Prices Could Pressure the Euro and Pound
data.description: According to Jin10, Mitsubishi UFJ analyst Derek Halpenny said in a report that the euro and pound could face downside risk if energy prices keep rising sharply and the conflict between the United States and Iran escalates further. He said higher natural gas prices would affect household income, which could weaken consumer spending and economic growth. Halpenny also said that although market expectations for future rate hikes by the Federal Reserve, the ECB, and the Bank of England are converging, investors may think the U.S. economy is better able to withstand potential monetary tightening.

data.title: Iranian Judiciary Chief Ejei: Iran Has No Hostility Toward Its Neighbors
data.description: According to Jin10, Iranian Judiciary Chief Ejei said Iran has no hostility or hatred toward its neighboring countries and considers them friends.

data.title: Bain Capital to Buy UK Supplements Firm Vitabiotics for $1.2 Billion
data.description:
Bain Capital agreed to acquire Vitabiotics Ltd., the British vitamins maker led by former Dragons' Den investor Tej Lalvani, according to Bloomberg.

The deal adds to a steady stream of UK takeovers this year.

data.title: STOCKS | Oracle Rises 1.4% Pre-Market After $7 Billion Pentagon Software Contract
data.description: Oracle rose 1.4% in pre-market trading after the Pentagon awarded the company a $7 billion software contract. According to Jin10, the stock was up 1.4% before the market opened.

data.title: ECB Survey Sees 2026 Core Inflation at 2.4%, Up From 2.2%
data.description: According to Jin10, the European Central Bank survey expected core inflation in 2026 to be 2.4%, up from 2.2% previously, and to remain unchanged at 2.2% in 2027.

data.title: STOCKS | MaxLinear Falls Nearly 11% Pre-Market on Weak Second-Quarter Results and Outlook
data.description: MaxLinear (MXL.O) fell nearly 11% in pre-market trading. According to Jin10, its second-quarter results and outlook were poor.

data.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guards Naval Base in Gilan Province Hit by U.S. Missile Strike
data.description: According to Iranian media, U.S. forces launched a missile strike on a naval base of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps in Gilan Province on July 24, and some equipment at the base was damaged. The report quoted Ali Bagheri, the deputy governor of Gilan Province, as saying the target was a Revolutionary Guard naval base in the province, with no casualty figures released.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Vietnam Weighs Limits on Social Media Use for Children Under 16
data.description:
Vietnam is considering restricting children under 16 from posting, commenting or reacting on social media as it moves toward tighter online protections for minors, according to Bloomberg.

The proposal would align Vietnam with other governments that have been increasing safeguards for children online.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese Leaders to Discuss AI Issues, China Says
data.description: Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesman Lin Jian said at a regular press briefing on the 24th that summit diplomacy plays an irreplaceable strategic guiding role in China-U.S. relations. According to Odaily, China is willing to work with the United States to implement the important consensus reached by the two heads of state in the field of artificial intelligence.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Malaysia May Return to Formula One if Bahrain Grand Prix Is Scrapped
data.description:
Malaysia could host a Formula One race for the first time in nine years if the Bahrain Grand Prix is dropped as the Middle East conflict continues, according to Bloomberg.

Youth and Sports Minister Mohammed Taufiq Johari said Malaysia could step in as a replacement, marking a possible return for the country to F1 after a nine-year absence.

data.title: Alphabet Target Price Cut to $405 by New Street Research
data.description: According to Jin10, New Street Research lowered Alphabet's target price from $455 to $405.

data.title: Chinese Banks Test Interbank Rates for New Loan Pricing
data.description:
Three of China's biggest banks have begun anchoring new loans to interbank rates, a move toward a pricing system that could better reflect supply and demand for money, according to Bloomberg.

The shift marks a break with the lending benchmark traditionally used for new credit and could signal wider changes in how banks price loans.
